source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
Wanda has lots of pages of square paper, whereby each page has an area of 4. She cuts each of the pages into right-angled triangles and squares (see the left hand diagram). She takes a few of these pieces and forms the shape in the right hand diagram. How big is the area of this shape?
<image1>

image_dependency: question_text explicitly refers to 'the left hand diagram' and 'the right hand diagram'
the target quantity is the area of a shape formed from pictured pieces, so the geometry is not recoverable from text alone
multi_step: solving likely requires identifying how the square pages are partitioned into triangles and squares, then mapping those pieces into the assembled shape
the problem asks for an area derived from rearranged pieces, implying at least decomposition plus recomposition/area aggregation
no answer choices are provided, so the solver likely needs constructive reasoning rather than recognition
verifiability: answer_type is numeric and answer_text='6', which is easy to check against a derived area
the underlying reasoning can in principle be verified by counting/combining piece areas from the diagram

decision_reasons: Core assets are complete: question text, required image, and numeric answer are all present.
Asset registry passed with no recorded issues, so the sample is usable for collection.
Although the problem is highly image-dependent, the needed diagram is available, so the core semantics are recoverable.
Initial scoring is strong on image dependency, multi-step reasoning, and verifiability, supporting inclusion at normal priority.
